duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentDAO.state() returns Succeeded when forVotes == getPastTotalSupply and both are 0, allowing vacuous proposal success and immediate execution without votes
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All eight threats describe the identical 0==0 vacuous truth bug in AgentDAO.state() enabling unauthorized proposal execution when token totalSupply is zero at snapshot. TECH-H001 has the most comprehensive technical analysis including the ContributionNft admin bypass vector and precise line references.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: FERC20.burnFrom/_burn decrements balances but never decrements _totalSupply, corrupting supply accounting and graduation token parameters
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All nine entries (including both instances of TECH2-H004) describe the same missing _totalSupply decrement in FERC20 burn functions. ECON-H002 provides the most comprehensive analysis of downstream impact including graduation supply mismatch, unwrapToken economics, and the race condition for late unwrappers.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Bonding.launch() always reverts due to nested nonReentrant modifier when calling launchFor(), forcing use of launchFor() with arbitrary creator attribution
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All nine entries (including both instances of TECH2-H005 and the second TECH2-H003 instance about Bonding.launch) describe the identical double-nonReentrant bug making launch() dead code and enabling creator spoofing via launchFor(). ECON-H005 best quantifies the economic impact including 99% tax revenue redirection.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentTax.dcaSell calculates minOutput based on input amount instead of expected output, providing incorrect slippage protection
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven threats describe the identical mathematical error in dcaSell where minOutput = (amountToSwap * (DENOM - slippage)) / DENOM uses input token amount as basis for output token slippage. TECH-H005 provides the clearest technical comparison with the correct implementation in _swapForAsset.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Deliberate VoteType (support=3) returns without setting hasVoted, enabling double-voting in governance
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe the same early return in GovernorCountingSimpleUpgradeable._countVote for Deliberate vote type that skips the hasVoted flag, allowing voters to cast again. TECH-H006 covers both GovernorCountingSimpleUpgradeable and GovernorCountingSimple variants and the AgentDAO score tracking interaction.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Genesis.participate enforces maxContributionVirtualAmount per-transaction not per-user, allowing whales to bypass the cap via repeated calls
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven threats (including first TECH2-H006 instance) describe the identical missing cumulative check in Genesis.participate where mapAddrToVirtuals[msg.sender] + virtualsAmt is never compared against maxContributionVirtualAmount. TECH-H007 has the clearest technical description of the check vs accumulation pattern.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ContributionNft.mint allows minting for non-succeeded proposals since it only checks proposalProposer without verifying proposal state
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All nine entries (including both instances of TECH2-H007 and the second TECH2-H005 instance about ContributionNft) describe the same missing isAccepted()/state check in ContributionNft.mint. OPS-H013 provides the best analysis of downstream integrity impacts on the contribution graph and reward systems, with correct inventory_refs.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Bonding.unwrapToken is public with no access control, allowing anyone to force-convert arbitrary users' FERC20 tokens to agent tokens without consent
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All six threats describe the same missing access control on unwrapToken allowing forced token conversion. TECH-H014 has the most comprehensive description including downstream tax/accounting consequences. Note: TECH2-H008 appears twice in input - one instance (about unwrapToken) is this duplicate, the other (about Genesis.onGenesisFailed) is a distinct unique threat. Due to shared ID, TECH2-H008 is not listed for removal.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Airdrop.airdrop uses assembly without validating _recipients.length == _amounts.length or sum(amounts) == _total, causing misdirected transfers and stranded tokens
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven threats describe input validation failures in the Airdrop assembly code. TECH-H010 comprehensively covers both sub-issues (mismatched array lengths causing garbage-address transfers AND _total vs sum(amounts) mismatch causing stranded tokens).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Bonding.buy() and sell() lack nonReentrant modifier at the Bonding contract level, with state updates occurring after external router calls
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe the same checks-effects-interactions violation in Bonding.buy/sell where tokenInfo state updates happen after router external calls without reentrancy protection. TECH-H011 best documents the config-dependent nature (requires callback-enabled tokens).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: BondingTax.swapForAsset uses manipulatable spot price from router.getAmountsOut for slippage protection, enabling flash loan sandwich attacks
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats describe the same spot-price-based slippage calculation in BondingTax.swapForAsset that can be manipulated via flash loans. ECON-H014 has the most detailed attack economics and highest severity rating (CRITICAL).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentDAO.getMaturity divides by forVotes without zero check, reverting when called by ServiceNft.mint for proposals with zero for-votes
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All four threats describe the same division-by-zero in getMaturity when forVotes==0, blocking ServiceNft.mint. ECON-H009 best documents the cascading failure combining the vacuous success bug with the division-by-zero to permanently brick the contribution pipeline.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ServiceNft.updateImpact is publicly callable and can zero out impact scores by re-invoking after mint when _coreServices already points to the current proposalId
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All three threats (including second TECH2-H007 instance about ServiceNft) describe the same public updateImpact griefing attack that zeros impact scores post-mint. ECON-H012 has the highest severity (CRITICAL) and clearest economic analysis of reward destruction.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: FGenesis.createGenesis is permissionless and automatically grants BONDING_ROLE on AgentFactoryV3 to each new Genesis contract
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same unrestricted role escalation via createGenesis granting BONDING_ROLE to user-deployed contracts. OPS-H006 has more detailed deployment evidence and privilege analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: AgentInference.promptMulti never updates prevAgentId in the loop, causing broken TBA caching and potential payment misrouting
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same missing prevAgentId update in the promptMulti loop. The second TECH2-H006 instance (about promptMulti) is the duplicate here. OPS-H017 is selected to avoid ID collision with the first TECH2-H006 (genesis cap, already removed in DUP-006).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: FRouter functions do not validate that factory.getPair returns a non-zero address, causing reverts on missing pairs
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both threats describe the same missing pair existence check in FRouter functions. OPS-H018 provides broader coverage of all affected functions.
